flags.concern: tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py:273 hardcodes `assert len(llm_call["tools"]) == 13`. Adding 3 new tools increases TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS to 16 items, so this assertion will fail. The plan must update the expected count to 16 (or remove the redundant hardcoded count since the preceding line already asserts `llm_call["tools"] == TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS`).
flags.evidence: Added an explicit step in Step 5 to update the hardcoded assertion `assert len(llm_call["tools"]) == 13` at line 273 of tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py to `16`, since FLAG-001 identified this as a guaranteed test failure when 3 new tools are added to TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS. Also noted the line-272 assertion already compares to the list itself, so the length check is redundant but updating it to 16 is the lowest-risk fix.
